frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with suture material visible and slit Penrose drain in trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears properly loaded, black marks visible on drain

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle at appropriate position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position at approximately 2/3 point from tip

frame_analyses.description: Needle approaching first mark on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Perpendicular approach angle, steady hand movement

frame_analyses.description: Needle driven through drain at first mark location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry appears close to mark, good technique

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pulled through drain, preparing for second mark
Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot with right hand leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw formation visible, proper hand positioning

frame_analyses.description: Second throw with left hand leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching observed, opposite direction from first throw

frame_analyses.description: Third throw with right hand leading again
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper alternation of hands between throws maintained

frame_analyses.description: Cutting both suture tails with scissors
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cuts leaving appropriate tail length

frame_analyses.description: Task completed, knot appears secure with drain slit closed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result shows good closure of drain slit

penalties.description: Entry point appears approximately 1mm from mark 1

confidence_rationale: Excellent visualization of knot tying sequence and hand switching. Minor uncertainty in exact suture deviation measurements due to camera angle at entry points.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation at mark 2 - partially obscured during needle passage
technique_summary: Expert-level performance with excellent time (72s vs 80-120s expert benchmark) and proper technique throughout. All required knot throws completed with correct hand alternation.
strengths: Excellent completion time within expert range
Proper surgeon's knot followed by alternating single throws
Clean suture cuts with appropriate tail length
improvement_suggestions: Minor refinement in needle entry precision to minimize deviation from marks
